active: A customer is considered 'active' if they had a successful money movement (transaction) in a given time period. Also called 'Transacting Active'. Use movement_date to aggregate distinct counts of customer_ids in the fact money movements table.
transacting_active: An account that has at least one financial transaction using any product or service within the app during a specified period.
mau: Monthly Active Users - Customers who have had at least one successful money movement in a given month.
established: A customer becomes 'established' when they have 3+ successful money movements in a 30-day window. Tracked in the ESTABLISHMENT_DATE field.
money_movement: Any successful transaction where money is transferred, including P2P payments, deposits, withdrawals, card purchases, direct deposits, crypto transactions, stock transactions, etc.
customer_token: Unique text identifier for a customer, typically prefixed with 'C_'. Primary key for identifying customers across tables.
p2p: Peer-to-Peer payments. The core feature allowing customers to send and receive money from other users.
cash_in: When a customer adds money directly into their wallet by linking an external bank account.
cash_out: When a customer transfers money from their balance to an external bank account or debit card.
debit_card: The app's debit card product. Customers can use it for purchases and ATM withdrawals.
direct_deposit: When a customer's paycheck or other income is deposited directly into their account.
pda: Paycheck Deposit Actives - Customers who have received $300+ in paychecks or income replacements via direct deposit in a single calendar month.
banking_state: Classification of customers based on product adoption hierarchy: PDA, Card Actives, P2P+Other Actives, or Inactive.
inflows: Total amount of money entering the platform across all channels.
inflows_per_active: Average amount of money each active customer brings into the platform within a set period.
gpv: Gross Payment Volume - Total dollar value of transactions processed.
idv: Identity Verification - Process where customers verify their identity by providing personal information. Required for certain features and higher transaction limits.
network_segment: Geographic segmentation capturing virality and network effects: Self-sustain (strongest), Invest (growing), Reassess (weakest).
cohort: Group of customers who joined or reached a milestone during the same time period. Used for retention analysis.
ltv: Lifetime Value - Predicted total profit a customer will generate over their lifetime with the product.
churn: When a customer stops using the product (becomes inactive). Measured as gross churn rate.
retention: Percentage of customers who remain active over time.
fact_money_movements: Core table containing all successful money activity movements. Located at YOUR_SCHEMA.FACT_MONEY_MOVEMENTS.
customer_activity_base: Key table containing monthly framework metrics at customer level with important segmentations. Located at YOUR_SCHEMA.CUSTOMER_ACTIVITY_BASE.
dim_customer_segmentations: Centralized table for customer demographics, behavioral, and geographic attributes. Located at YOUR_SCHEMA.DIM_CUSTOMER_SEGMENTATIONS.
customer_profile: Comprehensive customer profile table with identification, account status, demographics, device usage, payment methods, and risk indicators. Located at YOUR_SCHEMA.CUSTOMER_PROFILE.
metrics_store: Centralized repository for metrics with governance and semantic layer. Single source of truth for metrics.
attach_rate: Percentage of customers who adopt a specific product. Example: Banking attach rate = customers with banking products / total actives.
